While Botswana has arid regions, the presence of these specific rocky, layered hills is more typical of the more mountainous/plateau regions of Namibia or South Africa.

The combination of yellow outer edge lines and a double white center line is a standard regulatory feature in Namibia. This infrastructure, combined with the Landscape and Botanics agents' high-confidence identification of Namibian highlands and xeric scrub, makes this the most likely candidate.

Botswana utilizes the same road marking convention (yellow edges, white center) as Namibia. While the infrastructure is a perfect match, the Landscape agent notes the rocky layering is slightly less typical for Botswana than for Namibia.

South Africa shares the same left-hand traffic and road marking standards (yellow edges, white center). The infrastructure is consistent with the Northern Cape province, which mirrors the arid environment described by the council.

The combination of yellow edge lines (Regulatory) and the specific arid, rocky terrain (Landscape/Botany) strongly points to Southern Africa. While the road markings and landscape match the Northern Cape, the extreme lack of any roadside furniture or markers is slightly more characteristic of the more desolate Namibian or Botswanan corridors.
